model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Michigan holds a narrow but consistent statistical edge over Arizona across every key metric, ranking 2nd nationally in both KenPom and Torvik with an adjusted efficiency margin of +38.38 compared to Arizona's +37.56, driven by the nation's best defense (AdjD 88.4 vs Arizona's 89.7) while the offenses are virtually identical (AdjO 126.7 vs 127.2). The primary concern is Big Ten Player of the Year Yaxel Lendeborg's low ankle sprain suffered in the Big Ten Championship loss to Purdue, but he has played through it during the tournament and Michigan's depth—including Aday Mara, Elliot Cadeau, and Morez Johnson Jr.—provides a safety net even if Lendeborg is slightly limited. Arizona enters fully healthy with Big 12 Tournament championship momentum behind Jaden Bradley and Koa Peat, making this essentially a coin-flip between the #2 and #3 teams in the country, but Michigan's fractionally superior two-way profile and deeper roster construction under Dusty May give the Wolverines the slightest of edges in what projects as one of the tightest Final Four games in recent memory.

reasoningStep.title: National Semifinal 2: Arizona vs Michigan — Elite 1-seed clash
reasoningStep.summary: Michigan's marginally superior efficiency profile (KenPom #2 vs #3, AdjEM +38.38 vs +37.56) and the nation's best defense give them a razor-thin edge over a fully healthy Arizona squad, though Lendeborg's ankle sprain and Arizona's Big 12 Tournament championship momentum make this nearly a true coin-flip.
evidence: Michigan KenPom #2 (AdjEM +38.38, AdjO 126.7 8th, AdjD 88.4 #1) vs Arizona KenPom #3 (AdjEM +37.56, AdjO 127.2 7th, AdjD 89.7 #1 KenPom)
Michigan Torvik #2 (AdjOE 127.7 7th, AdjDE 90.5 2nd) vs Arizona Torvik #3 (AdjOE 126.2 10th, AdjDE 91.1 3rd)
Both teams 31-2; Arizona won Big 12 Tournament (Jaden Bradley MOP, buzzer-beater vs Iowa State); Michigan lost Big Ten final to Purdue
Michigan's Yaxel Lendeborg (Big Ten POY, 14.7/7.2/3.2) dealing with low ankle sprain from Big Ten Championship; expects to play but may be limited
Arizona fully healthy with Koa Peat back from February lower body injury; balanced scoring attack
Michigan's depth (Aday Mara, Elliot Cadeau, Morez Johnson Jr., Trey McKenney) showed ability to win without peak Lendeborg (71-67 vs Ohio State in Big Ten Tournament)

result.text: Michigan Star Yaxel Lendeborg Reveals Injury Status for NCAA Tournament - Detroit Sports Nation About Editorial Disclaimer Ethics Policy Fact-Checking & Corrections Policy Privacy Policy About Editorial Disclaimer Ethics Policy Fact-Checking & Corrections Policy Privacy Policy Search Home Detroit Lions Lions Roster Detroit Tigers Detroit Red Wings Detroit Pistons College Sports Home Detroit Lions Lions Roster Detroit Tigers Detroit Red Wings Detroit Pistons College Sports Home » Michigan Star Yaxel Lendeborg Reveals Injury Status for NCAA Tournament Michigan Star Yaxel Lendeborg Reveals Injury Status for NCAA Tournament By: Don Drysdale Published: Mar 15, 2026, 6:56 pm Modified: Mar 15, 2026, 6:56 pm The Michigan Wolverines appear to have avoided a major injury scare just days before the start of the 2026 NCAA Men’s Basketball Tournament . According to reporter Alejandro Zúñiga , Michigan forward Yaxel Lendeborg revealed that he is dealing with a low ankle sprain , but he expects to play in the Wolverines’ NCAA Tournament opener. That is welcome news for a Michigan team that earned a No. 1 seed in the Midwest Region after posting an impressive 31–3 record during the regular season. A Key Piece of Michigan’s Title Push Lendeborg has been one of the most important players on Michigan’s roster this season. The 6-foot-9 forward has delivered strong production on both ends of the floor and is a major reason why the Wolverines are considered a legitimate national championship contender. During the 2025–26 season , Lendeborg averaged: 14.4 points per game 7.0 rebounds 3.3 assists 50.6% shooting from the field 34.3% from three-point range 82% from the free-throw line His all-around impact helped him earn 2025–26 Big Ten Player of the Year honors , along with All-Big Ten recognition . Why His Health Matters Michigan’s tournament hopes depend heavily on the versatility Lendeborg brings to the lineup. His ability to score inside, stretch the floor, and facilitate offense makes him one of the most complete forwards in the tournament field. If the injury had been more serious, it could have dramatically altered Michigan’s outlook in the bracket. Instead, the early indications suggest the Wolverines will have their star available when March Madness begins. Leave a Reply Cancel reply Your email address will not be published. result.text: Michigan Star Yaxel Lendeborg Reveals Injury Status for NCAA Tournament - Detroit Sports Nation About Editorial Disclaimer Ethics Policy Fact-Checking & Corrections Policy Privacy Policy About Editorial Disclaimer Ethics Policy Fact-Checking & Corrections Policy Privacy Policy Search Home Detroit Lions Lions Roster Detroit Tigers Detroit Red Wings Detroit Pistons College Sports Home Detroit Lions Lions Roster Detroit Tigers Detroit Red Wings Detroit Pistons College Sports Home » Michigan Star Yaxel Lendeborg Reveals Injury Status for NCAA Tournament Michigan Star Yaxel Lendeborg Reveals Injury Status for NCAA Tournament By: Don Drysdale Published: Mar 15, 2026, 6:56 pm Modified: Mar 15, 2026, 6:56 pm The Michigan Wolverines appear to have avoided a major injury scare just days before the start of the 2026 NCAA Men’s Basketball Tournament . According to reporter Alejandro Zúñiga , Michigan forward Yaxel Lendeborg revealed that he is dealing with a low ankle sprain , but he expects to play in the Wolverines’ NCAA Tournament opener. That is welcome news for a Michigan team that earned a No. 1 seed in the Midwest Region after posting an impressive 31–3 record during the regular season. A Key Piece of Michigan’s Title Push Lendeborg has been one of the most important players on Michigan’s roster this season. The 6-foot-9 forward has delivered strong production on both ends of the floor and is a major reason why the Wolverines are considered a legitimate national championship contender. During the 2025–26 season , Lendeborg averaged: 14.4 points per game 7.0 rebounds 3.3 assists 50.6% shooting from the field 34.3% from three-point range 82% from the free-throw line His all-around impact helped him earn 2025–26 Big Ten Player of the Year honors , along with All-Big Ten recognition . Why His Health Matters Michigan’s tournament hopes depend heavily on the versatility Lendeborg brings to the lineup. His ability to score inside, stretch the floor, and facilitate offense makes him one of the most complete forwards in the tournament field. If the injury had been more serious, it could have dramatically altered Michigan’s outlook in the bracket. Instead, the early indications suggest the Wolverines will have their star available when March Madness begins. Leave a Reply Cancel reply Your email address will not be published. modelTrace.content:
Now I have a comprehensive picture of both teams. Let me compile my analysis:

**Arizona (1-seed, West):**
- KenPom #3: AdjEM +37.56, AdjO 127.2 (7th), AdjD 89.7 (#1 nationally)
- Torvik #3: AdjOE 126.2 (10th), AdjDE 91.1 (3rd)
- Record: 31-2, Big 12 Tournament champions
- Koa Peat fully healthy after returning from lower body injury in early March
- Jaden Bradley named Big 12 Tournament MOP (buzzer-beater vs Iowa State in semis)
- Balanced scoring, elite two-way team

**Michigan (1-seed, Midwest):**
- KenPom #2: AdjEM +38.38, AdjO 126.7 (8th), AdjD 88.4 (#1 nationally)
- Torvik #2: AdjOE 127.7 (7th), AdjDE 90.5 (2nd)
- Record: 31-2, Big Ten regular season champions (lost Big Ten Tournament final to Purdue)
- Yaxel Lendeborg (Big Ten Player of the Year) dealing with low ankle sprain from Big Ten Championship loss
- Key players: Lendeborg (14.7 ppg, 7.2 rpg, 3.2 apg), Aday Mara, Elliot Cadeau, Morez Johnson Jr., Trey McKenney
- Dusty May's remarkable rebuild

**Key comparison:**
- Michigan has a slight edge in overall efficiency: +38.38 vs +37.56 (0.82 points)
- Michigan's defense is marginally better: AdjD 88.4 vs 89.7 (1.3 points)
- Offenses are virtually identical: Michigan AdjO 126.7 vs Arizona 127.2
- Both teams are 31-2
- Michigan's Lendeborg ankle sprain is a concern but he's been playing through it for the tournament
- Arizona enters with Big 12 Tournament championship momentum; Michigan lost Big Ten final to Purdue

This is an incredibly tight matchup between the #2 and #3 teams in the country. Michigan holds a slight statistical edge across the board, but Lendeborg's ankle is a real concern at this stage. Arizona is fully healthy and riding championship momentum.

  "pick": {
    "gameId": "f4-2",
    "winnerId": "michigan",
    "confidence": 0.54,
    "rationale": "Michigan holds a narrow but consistent statistical edge over Arizona across every key metric, ranking 2nd nationally in both KenPom and Torvik with an adjusted efficiency margin of +38.38 compared to Arizona's +37.56, driven by the nation's best defense (AdjD 88.4 vs Arizona's 89.7) while the offenses are virtually identical (AdjO 126.7 vs 127.2). The primary concern is Big Ten Player of the Year Yaxel Lendeborg's low ankle sprain suffered in the Big Ten Championship loss to Purdue, but he has played through it during the tournament and Michigan's depth—including Aday Mara, Elliot Cadeau, and Morez Johnson Jr.—provides a safety net even if Lendeborg is slightly limited. Arizona enters fully healthy with Big 12 Tournament championship momentum behind Jaden Bradley and Koa Peat, making this essentially a coin-flip between the #2 and #3 teams in the country, but Michigan's fractionally superior two-way profile and deeper roster construction under Dusty May give the Wolverines the slightest of edges in what projects as one of the tightest Final Four games in recent memory."
